title: limnia spathulata
intro: Limnia spathulata, also known as Marsh Marigold, is a perennial plant that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to the presence of alkaloids and glycosides. The health benefits of Limnia spathulata include its ability to reduce swelling, alleviate pain, and support wound healing, making it a valuable treatment for skin conditions and injuries. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health issues, but it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it as a medicinal remedy.
